spec: f2b-replicate harness RunSpec — fresh-item replication + oracle-structure controls for the F2 verifier-offload signal (architecture-advisor frozen design). Frozen design record: registry/experiments/f2b-replicate.json. This manifest pins the harness INPUTS only; it may not change any frozen design field or threshold. PINNING DIRECTION NOTE (differs from F2): the frozen record pins THIS harness's staged-bytes manifest sha directly (pins.harness_manifest), so this file must NOT contain the record's frozen_sha256 (that would be circular); the record's hash lives in registry/frozen-index.json and in the record itself.

analysis_script.sha256: PINNED-IN-REGISTRY-RECORD (the frozen record carries the authoritative sha; the smoke test cross-checks the committed file against it)

pins.perItemRecordSha256: every d-qa-r item carries record_path + record_sha256; the verifier re-hashes the canonical record file at use and fails closed (ERR_RECORD_PIN) on mismatch — the shuffled verifier verifies the PERMUTED record's pin the same way

R1.pinned_by: registry/experiments/f2b-replicate.json pins.model_revisions (carried over from F2 amendment 2)

R3.pinned_by: registry/experiments/f2b-replicate.json pins.model_revisions (carried over from F2 amendment 3)

prm.pinned_by: registry/experiments/f2b-replicate.json pins.model_revisions (carried over from F2 amendment 3)
prm.note: HC3/EF3 trained-PRM comparator; real HFPRM backend identical to the F2 one (Modal-smoked by poc/modal/modal_prm_smoke.py on 2026-07-08)

shuffle.algorithm: Sattolo cyclic permutation over the sorted covered-concept urns via random.Random(perm_seed) — a derangement by construction (no concept maps to its own record); asserted fixed-point-free at build; the realized urn->record map + its sha256 are written to shuffle-map.json in the results directory and the sha is emitted on every shuffled-arm record
shuffle.note: the shuffled-kernel-verify-retry arm is the LOAD-BEARING oracle-structure control (advisor design): identical retry topology and cost, record CONTENT decoupled from the item

design_constants_from_frozen_record.note: copied VERBATIM from registry/experiments/f2b-replicate.json for runner convenience; the frozen record is authoritative. RIGHT-SIZED 2026-07-09 (advisor): 250 items, 3 seeds, single fixed retry budget k=4.

separation_gate.note: acc(R3-alone) - acc(R1-alone) >= 0.10 (5-seed mean) with one-sided 95% lower CI > 0, else ratio secondaries are INSTRUMENT-INVALID (absolute primary still reads)

arm_plan.prm_note: best-of-4 reranking (advisor right-size N=4); realized FLOPs on the F0 ledger; the matched-FLOPs PRM secondary compares ledgers, not intentions
arm_plan.retry_final_answer: last attempt (accepted or budget-exhausted); pre-declared, no post-hoc pick; identical rule for kernel-verify, shuffled-verify, gloss-self-verify and gold-oracle arms
arm_plan.gold_oracle_note: gold-oracle-retry DROPPED in the advisor right-size (2026-07-09): the NON-DEPLOYABLE diagnostic ceiling is cut from the arm set; the runner no longer emits gold cells and the pinned analysis resolves its (unreferenced) gold fields to null
arm_plan.ext_pipeline_fix: the F2 ext_vector defect is closed: the external d-ext slice runs through each arm's OWN pipeline (verify arms via run_verify_retry with the arm's verifier, text arm with its context block); d-ext items are not kernel-checkable so the verifier ABSTAINS off-coverage there — the vector measures deployed off-coverage behaviour, not transfer (transfer = the gated f2b-TRANSFER experiment)
arm_plan.shared_affordance: P10 IF-C constrained decoding for EVERY arm: answers produced by constrained selection over the pinned option set (A-D or yes/no) via sequence logprob; arms differ ONLY in verification instrument and context block
arm_plan.dropped_arms_note: R2, gold-oracle-retry, the k-ladder (single fixed k=4), cascades (HE2 failed confirmatory in F2 and is not re-litigated), int4, RAG and self-consistency are DROPPED per the advisor right-size; their F2 confirmatory readings stand

prompt_frames.qa_prefix: Answer the following question about the meaning of a word.

flop_accounting.formula: F0 section 3.3: FLOPs = 2*N_active*T_total + 2*L*T^2*d_attn per sequence; prefill and decode metered separately; T measured with the arm's own tokenizer
flop_accounting.cpu_note: CPU-side verifier work is metered as measured wall-clock seconds and converted at this pinned rate ONLY for the aggregate figure; wall-clock is reported separately (F0 section 3.3)
flop_accounting.usd_note: Modal list prices used for planning + $-per-query conversion; actual Modal billing reconciled from the dashboard at run time

planning.note: dry-plan ESTIMATES only — never emitted as measurements

mock.note: SYNTHETIC mechanics-only stub LM (no torch needed); every number it produces is labelled MOCK and never a measurement; the verifiers (true AND shuffled), extraction, gold-oracle gate, flop-meter and emitter code paths are the REAL ones

inputs_status.d-qa-r: BUILT + pinned 2026-07-09 (data/d-qa-r/build-dqar.py, generator seed dqar/1|f2b-replicate|20260709): 1000 FRESH covered items over the 108 covered concepts; ID-disjoint (dqar: namespace) AND full-prompt-surface-disjoint from all 650 d-qa items (LC8, fail-closed); leak-check PASS (data/d-qa-r/leak-check.json). RIGHT-SIZE (advisor 2026-07-09): the runner evaluates a fixed deterministic prefix of the rank-sorted covered set, per_arm_items=250; the full 1000-item corpus stays on disk and keeps its pinned kot-corpus-hash (no corpus re-pin), and every subset item remains fresh + disjoint by construction
inputs_status.d-qa: reused from F2 (amendment 1 pins) for two purposes ONLY: the pinned gloss corpus (text arms) and the original covered items the pinned d-xif labelled outputs reference (P10 gate re-verification); NO d-qa item is scored as an eval item in this experiment
inputs_status.d-xif: reused from F2 (amendment 5 pins): 500 covered real R1 outputs re-verified mechanically through THIS harness's extraction machinery (run_extraction_instrument_dxif); fail-closed pins
inputs_status.d-ext: reused from F2 (amendment 4 pins): 500 externally-authored OpenBookQA items; runs through the FIXED per-arm ext pipeline; descriptive raw vectors only (no endpoint) — items are not kernel-checkable, so this slice measures off-coverage abstention behaviour, not transfer
